description: You are a calculus professor preparing interactive course materials for a Calculus I unit on derivatives and critical points. Create a GeoGebra visualization for the function f(x) = x^3 - 3x + 1. The visualization must include the function f(x) and its derivative f'(x) plotted together, an interactive tangent line that moves along the curve, identified critical points where f'(x) = 0, and a display of numerical values showing the instantaneous rate of change at the moveable point. Save the file as 'derivative_explorer.ggb' in ~/Documents/GeoGebra/projects/
